summary: Crisp Miller soundboard captures a hot‑summer 1982 Starlight show with standout Half‑Step → Franklin’s, To Lay Me Down and a killer Shakedown Street jam.
review: The SBD from Miller delivers generally clear, punchy dead‑style mix (with the usual pitch/EQ quirks that require a little post‑processing), letting the band’s strong mid‑’80s form shine.  Highlights include an inspired Half‑Step Mississippi into Franklin’s Tower, soaring To Lay Me Down, a superb Shakedown Street/Samson sequence and one of the best Big Railroad Blues of the era; a few first‑set songs (Althea, Cassidy) are noted as weaker but overall the performance is consistently praised.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (30+) rate the show 4‑5 stars and extol the musical moments, while technical notes are mostly positive with only minor sound‑board imperfections; this yields a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s SBD is described as crisp and clear, but listeners note pitch/speed errors in the first set, occasional low‑bass roll‑off and a need for EQ tweaks.  Overall it is more listenable than many audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong but some early songs feel flat; the second set builds into a high‑energy sequence (Shakedown, Samson, Let It Grow) that many reviewers cite as the highlight of the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring solos on Half‑Step → Franklin’s, To Lay Me Down, Big Railroad and Shakedown Street; a few note his voice was a bit shot but the playing stayed tight.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally lauded for solid bass foundation and tight sync with Jerry; one reviewer calls his work on Cumberland "lackluster" while another notes he “drops bombs” on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned positively for his slide break in Minglewood Blues and steady rhythm throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keys) highlighted for fire‑ballooning jams on Shakedown Street, Samson & Delilah and a strong presence on Let It Grow.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming gets nods mainly through the “4‑song pre‑drums” second‑set sequence; no detailed criticism.
